doc_title: Pseudonymisation Under the GDPR — EDPB Guidelines 01/2025
label: partial
reason: EDPB Guidelines 01/2025 on Pseudonymisation is a real document and the legal substance is accurate, but the retrieved text is a brief paraphrase with invented section headings rather than authentic document text.
response_turn1:
The EDPB did adopt Guidelines 01/2025 on Pseudonymisation on 16 January 2025, and the substantive legal positions described (pseudonymised data remain personal data, GDPR continues to apply, Article 11 carve-out, Article 9 still applies to pseudonymised health data) are consistent with the actual guidelines. However, the retrieved text is a very short paraphrased summary rather than the actual document, and section headings like "Recital — Pseudonymisation Is Not Anonymisation" and "Health-Data Specifics" do not match the real document's structure.

LABEL: PARTIAL
CONFIDENCE: 78
REASON: EDPB Guidelines 01/2025 on Pseudonymisation is a real document with accurate substantive positions reflected here, but the retrieved text is a brief paraphrase with invented section headings rather than the authentic document text.
